title: nf-core/magmap pipeline parameters
description: nf-core/magmap is a bioinformatics best-practice analysis pipeline for mapping reads to a (large) collections of genomes.

input_output_options.title: Input/output options
input_output_options.description: Define where the pipeline should find input data and save output data.

input.description: Path to a file containing information about the samples in the experiment.
input.help_text: You will need to create a design file with information about the samples in your experiment before running the pipeline. Use this parameter to specify its location. It has to be a comma-separated file with 3 columns, and a header row.

genomeinfo.description: Path to a file containing information about the genomes to map to, see [usage docs](https://nf-co.re/magmap/usage#genome-input).

gtdb_metadata.description: Comma-separated list of paths to tab-separated files containing information from GTDB. For column description, see [usage docs](https://nf-co.re/magmap/usage#gtdb-metadata).
gtdb_metadata.help_text: Typically, the files are the standard GTDB metadata files, e.g. `bac120_metadata_r226.tsv.gz`. You will need to use or create a file with information about the genomes' taxonomy you want to map to before running the pipeline. Use this parameter to specify its location. It has to be a comma-separated file with 9 columns, and a header row. Columns are: accno, checkm_completeness, checkm_contamination, checkm_strain_heterogeneity, contig_count, genome_size, gtdb_genome_representative, gtdb_representative, gtdb_taxonomy.

checkm_metadata.description: Comma-separated list of path to tab-separated files containing the output from CheckM. For column description, see [usage docs](https://nf-co.re/magmap/usage#checkmcheckm2-metadata).
checkm_metadata.help_text: You will need to use or create a file with information about the genomes' statistics you want to map to before running the pipeline. Use this parameter to specify its location. It has to be a comma-separated file with 5 columns, and a header row. Columns are: checkm_completeness, checkm_contamination, contig_count, checkm_strain_heterogeneity, genome_size.

gtdbtk_metadata.description: Comma-separated list of paths to tab-separated files containing a .tsv file which has the same columns as the output from GTDB-Tk, see [usage docs](https://nf-co.re/magmap/usage#gtdb-tk-metadata).
gtdbtk_metadata.help_text: You will need to use or create a file with information about the genomes' taxonomy you want to map to before running the pipeline. Use this parameter to specify its location. It has to be a comma-separated file with 3 columns, and a header row. Columns are: gtdb_genome_representative, gtdb_representative, gtdb_taxonomy.

remote_genome_sources.description: Comma-separated list of paths to tsv files with information about genomes in NCBI 'assembly_summary' format. The default are GenBank and RefSeq summary files.
remote_genome_sources.help_text: The summary files need to contain the column '#assembly_accession', referring to genome identifiers used in sourmash indexes and 'ftp_path' pointing to where the genome can be fetched. The directory structure of the 'ftp_path' paths needs to conform with NCBI standards and contain a contig file with the pattern '<accno>*_genomic.fna.gz'.

genome_store_dir.description: Path to a directory where downloaded genome files is stored.
genome_store_dir.help_text: Any genome files downloaded by the pipeline will be stored in this directory for potential later use.

prokka_store_dir.description: Path to a directory where prokka output is stored.
prokka_store_dir.help_text: Prokka annotation files created by the pipeline will be stored in this directory for potential later use.

email.help_text: Set this parameter to your e-mail address to get a summary e-mail with details of the run sent to you when the workflow exits. If set in your user config file (`~/.nextflow/config`) then you don't need to specify this on the command line for every run.

multiqc_title.description: MultiQC report title. Printed as page header, used for filename if not otherwise specified.

outdir.description: The output directory where the results will be saved. You have to use absolute paths to storage on Cloud infrastructure.

skip_qc.description: Skip all QC steps except for MultiQC.

trimming_options.title: Trimming options
trimming_options.description: All the trim option are listed below

clip_r1.description: Instructs Trim Galore to remove bp from the 5' end of read 1 (or single-end reads).

clip_r2.description: Instructs Trim Galore to remove bp from the 5' end of read 2 (or single-end reads).

three_prime_clip_r1.description: Instructs Trim Galore to remove bp from the 3' end of read 1 AFTER adapter/quality trimming has been performed.

three_prime_clip_r2.description: Instructs Trim Galore to remove bp from the 3' end of read 2 AFTER adapter/quality trimming has been performed.

trim_reads.description: Instructs Trim Galore to apply the --nextseq=X option, to trim based on quality after removing poly-G tails.

save_trimmed.description: Save the trimmed FastQ files in the results directory.

mapping_options.title: Mapping options
mapping_options.description: All the options you can use for BBmap or BBduk)

sequence_filter.description: Instructs BBduk to use a fasta file to filter away sequences before running further analysis.

bbmap_ambiguous.description: Select how BBmap should treat ambiguous mapping

bbmap_save_index.description: Save ref folder containing the reference index

featurecounts_fraction.help_text: This parameter works best coupled with `--bbmap_ambiguous`. See documentation.

sourmash_options.title: Sourmash
sourmash_options.description: All the options available for Sourmash

skip_sourmash.description: Skip Sourmash filtering for user-provided genomes

institutional_config_options.title: Institutional config options
institutional_config_options.description: Parameters used to describe centralised config profiles. These should not be edited.
institutional_config_options.help_text: The centralised nf-core configuration profiles use a handful of pipeline parameters to describe themselves. This information is then printed to the Nextflow log when you run a pipeline. You should not need to change these values when you run a pipeline.

custom_config_version.description: Git commit id for Institutional configs.

custom_config_base.help_text: If you're running offline, Nextflow will not be able to fetch the institutional config files from the internet. If you don't need them, then this is not a problem. If you do need them, you should download the files from the repo and tell Nextflow where to find them with this parameter.

generic_options.title: Generic options
generic_options.description: Less common options for the pipeline, typically set in a config file.
generic_options.help_text:
These options are common to all nf-core pipelines and allow you to customise some of the core preferences for how the pipeline runs.

Typically these options would be set in a Nextflow config file loaded for all pipeline runs, such as `~/.nextflow/config`.

publish_dir_mode.description: Method used to save pipeline results to output directory.
publish_dir_mode.help_text: The Nextflow `publishDir` option specifies which intermediate files should be saved to the output directory. This option tells the pipeline what method should be used to move these files. See [Nextflow docs](https://www.nextflow.io/docs/latest/process.html#publishdir) for details.

email_on_fail.description: Email address for completion summary, only when pipeline fails.
email_on_fail.help_text: An email address to send a summary email to when the pipeline is completed - ONLY sent if the pipeline does not exit successfully.

plaintext_email.description: Send plain-text email instead of HTML.

max_multiqc_email_size.description: File size limit when attaching MultiQC reports to summary emails.

monochrome_logs.description: Do not use coloured log outputs.

hook_url.description: Incoming hook URL for messaging service
hook_url.help_text: Incoming hook URL for messaging service. Currently, MS Teams and Slack are supported.

multiqc_config.description: Custom config file to supply to MultiQC.

multiqc_logo.description: Custom logo file to supply to MultiQC. File name must also be set in the MultiQC config file

multiqc_methods_description.description: Custom MultiQC yaml file containing HTML including a methods description.

validate_params.description: Boolean whether to validate parameters against the schema at runtime

pipelines_testdata_base_path.description: Base URL or local path to location of pipeline test dataset files

trace_report_suffix.description: Suffix to add to the trace report filename. Default is the date and time in the format yyyy-MM-dd_HH-mm-ss.

help_full.description: Display the full detailed help message.

show_hidden.description: Display hidden parameters in the help message (only works when --help or --help_full are provided).
